description: Nepali contrastive stress and emphasis test pairs for TTS benchmark evaluation (NepTTS-Bench). Tests whether a TTS system can correctly shift prosodic emphasis to change meaning or pragmatic focus, modeled after StressTest and EmphAssess benchmarks for English.

notes.usage: Each entry tests prosodic emphasis placement. Use variations to evaluate whether TTS produces audibly distinct stress patterns that convey the intended pragmatic meaning.
notes.evaluation: A good TTS system should produce perceptibly different prosodic contours when different words carry contrastive stress. Listeners should be able to identify which word is emphasized.
notes.emphasis_marking: Emphasized words are marked with ** ** in sentence_with_emphasis fields. In actual TTS input, emphasis may be conveyed via SSML <emphasis> tags or prosodic markup.
notes.coverage: Covers focus/emphasis shift (फोकस परिवर्तन), structurally ambiguous contrastive pairs (विरोधाभासी जोडी), and corrective emphasis (सुधारात्मक बल)
notes.romanization: Romanization follows standard Nepali transliteration conventions used in linguistics

variations.pragmatic_meaning: It was RAM who bought the book (not someone else)
Ram bought a BOOK (not something else)
Ram BOUGHT the book (didn't borrow or steal it)
It was SITA who cooked the rice (not someone else)
Sita cooked RICE (not dal or vegetables)
Sita COOKED the rice (didn't just wash it)
It was HARI who drove the car yesterday (not someone else)
Hari drove the car YESTERDAY (not today or the day before)
Hari drove a CAR yesterday (not a motorcycle or bicycle)

focus_emphasis_shift.english: Mother brought vegetables from the market.

variations.pragmatic_meaning: It was MOTHER who brought the vegetables (not father or someone else)
Mother brought vegetables from THE MARKET (not from the garden or neighbor)
Mother brought VEGETABLES from the market (not fruit or meat)

focus_emphasis_shift.english: Elder brother is going to Kathmandu tomorrow.

variations.pragmatic_meaning: Elder brother is going to Kathmandu TOMORROW (not today or next week)
Elder brother is going to KATHMANDU tomorrow (not Pokhara or Biratnagar)
HE gave me money (not someone else)
He gave ME money (not you or someone else)
He gave me MONEY (not a gift or food)

focus_emphasis_shift.english: The children play in the garden.

variations.pragmatic_meaning: It's the CHILDREN who play in the garden (not adults)
The children play IN THE GARDEN (not on the road or at school)

focus_emphasis_shift.english: The teacher gave the student a book.

variations.pragmatic_meaning: It was the TEACHER who gave the student a book (not the principal)
The teacher gave the STUDENT a book (not the parent or colleague)
The teacher gave the student a BOOK (not a pen or notebook)

focus_emphasis_shift.english: I will go to school tomorrow.

variations.pragmatic_meaning: I will go to school tomorrow (not you or someone else)
I will go to school TOMORROW (not today or day after)
I will go to SCHOOL tomorrow (not the office or market)

focus_emphasis_shift.english: Elder sister wore a red sari.

variations.pragmatic_meaning: It was ELDER SISTER who wore the red sari (not mother or younger sister)
Elder sister wore a RED sari (not green or blue)
Elder sister wore a red SARI (not a kurta or dress)
It was FATHER who cooked food at home (surprisingly, not mother)
Father cooked food AT HOME (not at the restaurant or office)
They built THREE houses (not two or four)
They built three HOUSES (not shops or temples)

variation_a.english: The son of the big man (ठूलो modifies physical size of मान्छे)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on ठूलो singles it out as an adjective modifying the man's physical attribute

variation_b.english: The son of the important person (ठूलो मान्छे as a compound meaning 'big shot')
variation_b.description: Emphasis on मान्छे treats ठूलो मान्छे as a compound meaning an important or influential person

variation_a.english: The story of a new Nepal (नयाँ modifies नेपाल)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on नयाँ implies a transformed or changed Nepal

variation_b.english: A new story about Nepal (नयाँ modifies कथा)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on नेपालको shifts नयाँ to modify the story rather than Nepal

variation_a.english: The toy belonging to the small/young child (सानो modifies बच्चा)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on सानो indicates the child's age or size

variation_b.meaning: the small toy for a child
variation_b.english: A small toy for children (सानो modifies खेलौना)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on बच्चाको shifts the adjective to modify the toy's size

variation_a.english: Ram said that [he/someone] hit Shyam (रामले is the subject of भन्यो)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on रामले clarifies Ram as the sayer — Ram reported the hitting

variation_b.english: Ram said that Shyam was HIT (emphasis on the act of hitting, not the saying)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on मार्यो highlights the violent action as the new/important information

variation_a.english: The house of my old/longtime friend (पुरानो modifies साथी)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on पुरानो means a friend from a long time ago

variation_b.english: The old house of my friend (पुरानो modifies घर)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on साथीको shifts पुरानो to describe the age of the house

variation_a.english: The girl with dark/black eyes (काली modifies आँखा)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on काली describes the eye color

variation_b.meaning: the dark girl with notable eyes
variation_b.english: The dark/beautiful girl known for her eyes (काली could modify केटी, or the whole phrase is a unit)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on आँखाकी makes the eyes the defining characteristic

variation_a.english: She likes READING (emphasis on the activity of reading, contrasting with other activities)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on पढ्न contrasts reading with other possible activities

variation_b.english: She genuinely ENJOYS reading (emphasis on the liking, not just doing it as obligation)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on मन highlights genuine enjoyment rather than mere habit

contrastive_pairs.english_base: We met the people who had just arrived.

variation_a.meaning: We met the people who had JUST arrived
variation_a.english: We met the people who had JUST NOW arrived (emphasis on the recency)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on भरखर stresses how recently they arrived

variation_b.meaning: We met THE PEOPLE who had just arrived
variation_b.english: We met the PEOPLE (specific ones) who had just arrived (emphasis on who was met)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on मान्छेलाई highlights the identity of those who were met

variation_a.meaning: a person who speaks nicely / tells good stories
variation_a.english: A person who says good things (राम्रो modifies कुरा — good talk)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on राम्रो makes it an adjective for कुरा, meaning good conversation

variation_b.meaning: a good person who talks / a person good at talking
variation_b.english: A person who is good at talking (राम्रो modifies the whole predicate or the person)
variation_b.description: Emphasis on गर्ने shifts the scope so that the person's skill at conversation is highlighted

variation_a.english: The father of three boys (तीन specifies the count of boys)
variation_a.description: Emphasis on तीन highlights the number of sons

variation_b.meaning: the father who has boys (three of them, specifically boys not girls)
variation_b.english: The father of boys (three of them) — emphasis on them being boys
variation_b.description: Emphasis on केटाका highlights the gender of the children rather than the count

corrective_emphasis.description: Corrects the agent: Gita, not Sita, cooked the rice
Corrects the destination: Pokhara, not Kathmandu
Corrects the object: a bicycle, not a motorcycle

corrective_emphasis.english_b: No, I came the DAY BEFORE YESTERDAY.
corrective_emphasis.description: Corrects the time: day before yesterday, not yesterday
Corrects the action: bought, not stole
Corrects the number: three, not five
Corrects the subject: I am going, not you
Corrects the object: milk, not tea
Corrects the location: at home, not at the office

corrective_emphasis.english_b: No, elder sister LISTENED TO the song.
corrective_emphasis.description: Corrects the action: listened, not sang
